exam_info.exam_name: Multi Tasking Non Technical Staff and Havaldar CBIC and CBN Examination 2023
exam_info.subject: MTS Non Tech Havaldar CBIC and CBN Examination 2023

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 30 days and 40 days, respectively. They began the work together but after a certain time, A leaves off and B completed the remaining work in 25 days. After how many days did A leave?
6 skilled workers can complete a work in 4 days. After three days 3 more skilled workers joined them. How many days will they now take to complete the remaining work?

In a class of boys and girls, the number of girls is twice the number of boys. If 20 more boys join the class, the ratio of girls and boys is 1:1. How many total students were there in the class initially?
Atul sells goods to a customer at a profit of P%. If the cost price of the goods for the customer is ₹1,50,000 and Atul makes a profit of ₹30,000, then what is the value of P?
Mixture 1 with a volume of 8 litres has 80% milk and Mixture 2 with a volume of 6 litres has 60% milk. Both the mixtures are mixed. Find the percentage of milk in the final mixture.

Study the given table and answer the question that follows.
The table shows the number of students appeared and passed in different fields in a University.

Years | Medical (Appeared/Passed) | Engineering (Appeared/Passed) | Commerce (Appeared/Passed) | Management (Appeared/Passed)
2010 | 300/250 | 460/256 | 380/352 | 365/322
2011 | 345/320 | 440/365 | 401/354 | 314/314
2012 | 280/240 | 384/327 | 460/400 | 378/344
2013 | 310/230 | 390/290 | 430/392 | 387/353
2014 | 450/380 | 415/358 | 388/352 | 270/227
2015 | 380/286 | 486/456 | 366/315 | 290/257

What percentage of total students appearing in 2011, appeared in the medical field?

A thief is spotted by a policeman from a distance of 250 m. When the policeman starts chase, the thief also starts running. Assuming that the speed of the thief is 10 km/h and that of the policeman is 12.5 km/h. How far will the thief run before he is caught?
Amit drove for 6 hours at a speed of 60 miles per hour and for 6 hours at 40 miles per hour. What was his average speed (in miles per hour) for the whole journey?

A jewel was sold passing through 3 hands and on the whole, its price is raised by 80%. The first seller earns 20% profit and the second seller earns 25% profit, respectively. Find the percentage of profit earned by the third seller.

A certain sum is invested at a simple interest rate of 12% per annum for a certain period of time. If the sum becomes four times the principal at the end of the period, what is the duration of the investment?

Mr. Kalra borrowed ₹1,35,000 to meet the expenses of his son's education. If the rate of interest is 12% per annum compounded annually, then how much amount will he have to make after 2 years and 3 months to settle the account?
The ratio of two numbers is 7:11 and their LCM is 462. What is the difference between these two numbers?
The diameter of the base of a cylindrical vessel is 30 cm and its height is 56 cm. Find its curved surface area (in cm²).
The length, breadth and height of a cuboid are in the ratio of 6:4:3. If the total surface area of the cuboid is 972 cm², what is the length (in cm) of the diagonal of the cuboid?
Three numbers are in the ratio 1:2:3. By adding 5 to each of the numbers then the ratio changes to 2:3:4. The smallest number is:

The following pie-charts show the distribution of students of graduate and post-graduate levels in five different institutes in a city.
Graduate level: Total = 284000; A=35%, B=20%, C=25%, D=15%, E=5%
Post-graduate level: Total = 240000; A=30%, B=20%, C=18%, D=22%, E=10%

What is the total number of students from institutes A, C and E at the graduate level and from institutes B and D at the post-graduate level?
The distance between two towns is covered in 9 hours at a speed of 60 kmph. By how much should the speed (in km/h) be increased so that 1 hour of the travelling time is saved?

A man decided to cover 7.5 km in 72 minutes. He decided to cover two thirds of the distance at 5 km/h and the remaining at some different speed. Find the speed after two third distance has been covered.
Rickey spends 35% of his salary on groceries, 15% on loans, 14% on electricity bills and 6% on miscellaneous. If his savings at the end of a month are ₹2,400, then his salary per month (in ₹) is:

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which conclusion/s logically follow/s from the given statements.

Statements: All trucks are bikes. All bikes are carts. Some carts are pens.
Conclusion I: Some trucks are carts.
Conclusion II: No bikes are pens.

What will come in the place of '?' in the following equation, if '+' and '−' are interchanged and '×' and '+' are interchanged?
357 × 7 + 19 ÷ 2 − 38 = ?
RN 12 is related to PL 16 in a certain way. In the same way, EJ 17 is related to CH 21. To which of the following is FL 15 related to following the same logic?

Which figure should replace the question mark (?) if the following figure series were to be continued?
[Figure series with X, B, T, L, 9, ☆ pattern]

options.a: Option with L, T, B, ☆, X, 9
options.b: Option with X, B, 9, L, T, ☆
options.c: Option with ☆, T, B, L, 9, X
options.d: Option with L, T, ☆, X, B, 9

questions.question:

PYJ_ S_LM _YNM YY_M B_RM

What should come in place of '?' in the given series?
3  21  111  561  2811  ?

Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series, will complete the series.
_FFEF__FF_F_

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which conclusion/s logically follow/s from the given statements.

Statements: Some chargers are metal. All metal are hard. Some hard are stools.
Conclusion I: All chargers are metal.
Conclusion II: Some stools are hard.

Kiran, Pihu, Siya, Leena, Reena and Jiya are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. Kiran sits 3rd to the right of Pihu. Siya sits 2nd to the right of Kiran. Leena sits 3rd to the left of Siya. Reena is an immediate neighbour of Leena.
Who sits to the immediate left of Jiya?

If 'I' stands for '+', 'J' stands for '×', 'K' stands for '÷' and 'L' stands for '−', what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
(95 K 19) J 7 L 15 I (10 J 4) = ?

Select the option figure in which the given figure (X) is embedded as its part (rotation is NOT allowed).
[Embedded figure question with an arrow-like shape]
428 is related to 317 following a certain logic. Following the same logic, 285 is related to 174. To which of the following is 597 related to, following the same logic?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.
(NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking the numbers into its constituent digits.)
(14, 42, 7)
(32, 34, 5)
If 5th May 2010 is Wednesday, then what will be the day of the week on 5th June 2022?

What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?
YFD, VJA, SNX, PRU, ?

What should come in place of ? in the given series?
26  37  59  92  136  ?

Select the correct mirror image of the given figure when the mirror is placed at MN as shown below.
[Figure: Kg4b2a with vertical mirror line MN]

What should come in place of '?' in the given series?
14  85  511  3067  18403  ?
In a certain code language, 'NAIL' is coded as '2637' and 'LANE' is coded as '3278'. What is the code for 'E' in the given code language?
In a certain code language, 'MENTOR' is coded as 'EMOUPS' and 'OCTAGON' is coded as 'COUBHPO'. What is the code for 'PENCIL' in the given code language?
Identify the vector quantity among these listed physical quantities?
'The Palace of Illusions', a rendition of the Hindu epic 'Mahabharata', was written by which of the following novelists?
G Krishnakumar was appointed chairman of which of the following in 2023?

options.a: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited

questions.question: ______________ was the first Indian woman to write an autobiography and also the first to do so in Bengali in her book titled 'Aamar Jiban', which was published in 1876.
Where is the Vivekanand Rock Memorial situated?
Under which Act was the rule of British Crown established over India?
The Kolar region of Karnataka is famous for which mineral from the following?

Match the following performances of Indian naatya, which were presented by the means of variegated components:
1) Angika  — (a) costumes and make-up
2) Vachika — (b) depiction of mental or psychological states
3) Aharya  — (c) spoken words
4) Sattvika — (d) gestures and other bodily movements
In Fencing, a contest between the two fencers is known as:
In which year has the Government of India instituted Arjuna Award to recognise outstanding achievement in national sports events?

'Atla Taddi', a festival celebrated by married and unmarried women alike to seek the blessings of Devi Gauri for a long and happy married life and a suitable groom, respectively, is celebrated in ____________.
PT Usha was elected as the president of which association in December 2022?

नीति आयोग (NITI Aayog) की पहली बहुआयामी गरीबी सूचकांक (MPI) 2021 रिपोर्ट के अनुसार, भारत में कौन-सा राज्य पहले स्थान पर है? / According to NITI Aayog's first Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) 2021 report, which state ranks first in India?
What is the full form of UPSC?
To which of the state does Sattriya classical dance belong?
Who among the following was a renowned dancer and the first person from Odisha to receive 'Padma Vibhushan' in 2000?
Which musical instrument does N Rajam play?
Which of the following is NOT a function of money?
According to the Census of India 2011, what was the population density recorded in Bihar?
Carbohydrates are utilised by the human body in the form of:
Which Indian musician is associated with the tabla?
On whose advice did Gandhiji spend a year travelling around British India to know the people of India?
कौन-सा घाटा सरकार की ऋण-ग्रहण आवश्यकताओं को दर्शाता है? / Which deficit represents the borrowing requirements of the government?
Which of the following fundamental duties was added to the Indian Constitution by the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002?

options.a: To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem
options.b: To protect and to improve the natural environment including forests, lakes, rivers and wildlife, and to have compassion for living creatures
options.c: To provide opportunities for education to his/her child or, as the case may be, ward between the age of six and fourteen years
options.d: To cherish and follow the noble ideals which inspired our national struggle for freedom

questions.question: शैल कायांतरण के दौरान निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सी चट्टानें बनती हैं? / Which of the following rocks are formed during metamorphism of rocks?

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam was a great man with a high level of ________.

The following sentence contains some errors. Select the option that correctly rectifies the error.
The forses that genarate conditions conduciive to crime and riiots are stronger in urban communities than in rurral areas.

options.a: The forsec that genarate conditions conduciive to crime and riots are stronger in urban communities than in rurall areas.
options.b: The forces that genereta conditions conducieve to crime and riotts are stronger in urban communities than in rural areas.
options.c: The forces that generate conditions conducive to crime and riots are stronger in urban communities than in rural areas.
options.d: The forces that genarete conditions conduciive to crime and riots are stronger in urban communities than in ruraal areas.

I found my teacher's points casual and informal in the group discussion.

Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.
One who is all powerful.

Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence.
Ramesh is owning a beautiful house in Noida.

Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.
When Nisha will have finished her task?

Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence.
He push the bushes aside and went a little farther.

Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.
Copying someone else's work and trying to submit it as your own

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
Beauty of it

Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word.
The girl was manacled and locked in the room.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.
Addiction of drugs proved detrimental for his health.

Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.
Plunge
Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.

Select the most appropriate option that can rectify the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to rectify it, select 'no rectification needed'.
I used a variety of sources to find the more cheap flight.

Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence.
All the members of my family are planning to visit Jammu and Kashmir this Novembur.

Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.
Ravi will go to the England.

Select the most appropriate response in the given situation of the eve of a battle to fill in the blank.
The commander: 'Honourable Emperor, all the troops have lined up for the battle and are awaiting your orders.' Emperor Chandragupta Maurya: '______________________.'

options.b: You should learn how to talk to an emperor.
options.d: We are ready for the pilgrimage.

questions.question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.
We prefer going to movies once in a blue moon.

The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error.
My father told me / that in his childhood / he was walking / to school every day.

Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.
Today's question paper felt like a cakewalk.

Comprehension (Blank 1): Modern football (1)________ in Britain in the 19th century. Since before medieval times, 'folk football' games had been played in towns and villages according to local customs and with a (2)_______ of rules. Industrialisation and (3)________, which reduced the amount of leisure time and space available to the working class, combined with a history of legal prohibitions against particularly (4)________ and destructive forms of folk football to undermine the game's status from the early 19th century onward. However, football was taken up as a winter game between residence houses at public schools such as Winchester, Charterhouse and Eton. Each school had its own rules; some allowed limited handling of the ball and others did not. The (5)_________in rules made it difficult for public schoolboys entering university to continue playing except with former schoolmates.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.

Comprehension (Blank 2): [Same passage as Q21]
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.

Comprehension (Blank 3): [Same passage as Q21]
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.

Comprehension (Blank 4): [Same passage as Q21]
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.

Comprehension (Blank 5): [Same passage as Q21]
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
